reasoning: This is the Eakin-Nagata theorem. The Lean formalization correctly captures all components: (1) `A : Subring B` with `[CommRing B]` encodes "A ⊂ B commutative rings" since a Subring of a CommRing automatically inherits a CommRing structure via Mathlib's `SubringClass.toCommRing` instance; (2) `Module.Finite A B` is definitionally `(⊤ : Submodule A B).FG`, which is exactly "B is finitely generated as an A-module" (the Module A B structure comes from the standard Subring Module instance); (3) `[IsNoetherianRing B]` matches "B is Noetherian"; (4) the conclusion `IsNoetherianRing A` matches "A is Noetherian". The types and hypothesis structure exactly mirror the informal statement with no added or dropped hypotheses.
